completeness.notes: Core pipeline requirements captured well; GenAI model selection criteria, image quality standards, and packaging text rendering requirements not elicited during discovery

technical_soundness.notes: Requirements that were captured are technically precise and measurable; acceptance criteria are specific

clarity.notes: Well-structured, priority tiers clear, acceptance criteria specific

feasibility.notes: PoC scope is realistic for 4-6 hours; must_have vs nice_to_have separation appropriate

security.notes: IAM + SSE-S3 captured; no model output security or content policy considerations

overall.notes: Solid requirements foundation with meaningful gaps in GenAI-specific discovery

findings.finding: AI model selection criteria never elicited during requirements discovery. The question 'which GenAI model and why' was never posed to the stakeholder, causing the architecture to default to the AWS-native Nova Canvas without evaluating packaging-specific quality requirements (text rendering accuracy, photorealism, prompt adherence for label copy).
findings.recommendation: Add a dedicated 'GenAI Component Probe' question set to the /requirements skill for any image generation engagement: (1) What image quality level is required — photorealistic product photography, illustration, or schematic? (2) Are there text rendering requirements — e.g., product name and attributes must be legible on the label? (3) What evaluation criteria matter most — cost, quality, latency, text adherence? (4) Provider constraints — AWS-native only, or any Bedrock model including Stability AI?

findings.finding: Image quality standards for packaging not captured. FR-003 accepts 'any model of choice' without specifying a quality bar. A packaging use case has specific quality requirements: label text must be legible and accurate, product photography must look commercially viable, cultural imagery must be contextually appropriate. Without a quality success criterion, the PoC could pass acceptance testing with unusable outputs.
findings.recommendation: Add SC-006 as a must_have success criterion: 'Image Quality Gate — at least 80% of generated images pass visual inspection for label text legibility and commercial viability.' Add packaging-specific acceptance criteria to FR-003: 'Generated product name and attribute text is legible on the label at 1:1 aspect ratio.'

findings.finding: AWS region preference never elicited. data_residency.required_regions defaults to us-east-1, which limits model availability. Stability AI premium models (SD3.5 Large, Stable Image Ultra) are available in us-west-2 (Oregon). This constraint was never surfaced during discovery and led to an architecture that initially missed the best-fit models.
findings.recommendation: Add 'AWS region preference' to constraints discovery questions. Note that Stability AI Bedrock models require us-west-2 or cross-region inference via 'us.' model ID prefix. us-west-2 also has a higher renewable energy percentage (sustainability benefit).

findings.finding: No benchmark evaluation criteria captured for the GenAI component. Success criteria SC-001 through SC-005 measure pipeline mechanics but not output quality. There is no quality gate that would catch a fundamentally poor model choice. For an interview context where the panel (Adobe senior engineers) will evaluate architectural decisions, this gap reduces the engagement's persuasive strength.
findings.recommendation: Add model evaluation criteria as a non-functional requirement: 'GenAI model must demonstrate acceptable image quality for packaging standards at PoC scale — assessed by human visual review before submission.' Reference benchmark methodology (TIFA, ImageReward) in architecture decisions rationale.

findings.finding: Production scaling path (hundreds of SKUs/month) not linked to the performance NFRs. throughput_rps is 1, which is correct for PoC, but the production scenario mention lacks a note on the parallelization strategy identified in architecture.json (concurrent.futures ThreadPoolExecutor).
findings.recommendation: Add note to non_functional_requirements.scalability cross-referencing the parallelization opportunity in architecture (concurrent.futures, 3-4 concurrent Bedrock calls). This strengthens the production extensibility narrative for the interview panel.

improvement_plan.action: Add GenAI Model Probe question set to /requirements skill
Add image quality success criterion (SC-006) as must_have
Add AWS region preference discovery question
Add packaging-specific acceptance criteria to FR-003 and FR-005

key_changes: Baseline assessment — RF-001 through RF-005 identified; model selection gap and image quality standard gap are P1 findings
Architecture updated to SD3.5 Large (addresses root cause of RF-001 — model selection without quality evaluation)
Region updated to us-west-2 (addresses RF-003)
RF-005 deferred as non-blocking
SA agent improvement plan written to .claude/plans/sa-agent-improvements.md addressing RF-001 and RF-002 systemically
RF-004 addressed via quality gate addition in improvement plan backlog
Remaining open findings (RF-002, RF-004) tracked in improvement plan as backlog items

completeness.notes: All 8 components defined with inputs/outputs; 5 data flows; 3 Mermaid diagrams included; WA 6-pillar scoring complete

technical_soundness.notes: Architecture is coherent but primary model (Nova Canvas) is not optimal for packaging label text rendering; alternatives table references legacy SDXL 1.0 rather than current-generation SD3.5 Large or Stable Image Ultra

well_architected.notes: 6-pillar WA scoring present with strengths and gaps per pillar; Reliability at 6/10 is the lowest pillar — retry logic and model fallback not implemented in component design

clarity.notes: Component table, data flows, Mermaid diagrams all clear and internally consistent

feasibility.notes: Architecture is achievable in 4-6 hours; all components are well-scoped; Pillow text overlay is the most uncertain element

overall.notes: Solid architecture with one high-impact correction needed in model selection

findings.finding: Primary model selection (Nova Canvas, $0.04/image) made without benchmark evidence for packaging-specific quality requirements. For use cases where product labels must render specific text accurately (product name, attributes, regulatory copy), Stable Diffusion 3.5 Large (8.1B parameters, $0.08/image) demonstrates superior text/prompt adherence — the critical dimension for packaging automation. The architecture's rationale cites 'superior image quality' for Nova Canvas without benchmark citation. Nova Canvas benchmarks (TIFA: 0.897, ImageReward: 1.250) are strong on general metrics, but SD3.5 Large's architecture specifically optimizes typography and complex prompt adherence, which matters for label copy fidelity.
findings.recommendation: Change primary model to Stable Diffusion 3.5 Large (stability.sd3-5-large-v1:0, $0.08/image). Retain Nova Canvas (amazon.nova-canvas-v1:0, $0.04/image) as cost-optimized secondary for rapid iteration. Retain Titan Image Generator V2 (amazon.titan-image-generator-v2:0, $0.01/image) as cheapest dev/test fallback. Cross-region inference via us.stability.sd3-5-large-v1:0 enables use from any US region.

findings.finding: Alternatives table rejects 'Stability AI SDXL via Bedrock' — a 2023-era legacy model that has been superseded by SD3.5 Large (Dec 2024) and Stable Image Ultra. Comparing Nova Canvas against SDXL creates a false impression that Nova Canvas has no peer-quality alternatives at the same price point. An Adobe interview panel with image generation expertise is likely to notice this gap.
findings.recommendation: Replace the SDXL entry with: (1) Stable Diffusion 3.5 Large — current-generation, superior text adherence, $0.08/image, why it IS the new primary; (2) Stable Image Ultra — premium photorealism, $0.08/image, optimal for hero product photography; (3) SDXL 1.0 as legacy-rejected option. This demonstrates current-generation awareness.

findings.finding: Architecture specifies us-east-1 exclusively. Stability AI models on Bedrock are available in us-west-2. The architecture doesn't document cross-region inference via the 'us.' model ID prefix, creating an undocumented dependency that could block model access on first setup — especially critical for G-001 (Bedrock Access Gate, which is BLOCKING).
findings.recommendation: Update primary region to us-west-2 (also higher renewable energy %, sustainability benefit). Document cross-region inference alternative: model ID us.stability.sd3-5-large-v1:0 allows cross-region invocation from us-east-1. Add to G-001 gate criteria: 'Stability AI model access confirmed in us-west-2 or via cross-region inference.'

findings.finding: Reliability pillar is the lowest WA score (6/10). The architecture's own recommendations identify retry/backoff and model fallback as gaps, but C-005 (Image Generator) component design does not include these behaviors in its specification. The 3-tier fallback pattern (primary → secondary → dev) is described at architecture level but not reflected in component inputs/outputs.
findings.recommendation: Update C-005 component design to explicitly list: (1) 3-tier model fallback logic (SD3.5L → Nova Canvas → Titan V2), (2) botocore adaptive retry config (max_attempts=3, mode=adaptive), (3) local /tmp checkpoint before S3 upload. Raise Reliability WA estimate from 6/10 to 7/10 post-implementation.

findings.finding: --dry-run flag appears in WA Operational Excellence recommendations but is absent from C-001 (CLI Entry Point) inputs list. Minor inconsistency between WA recommendations and component specification.
findings.recommendation: Add '--dry-run' to C-001 inputs: 'CLI flags (--model, --region, --dry-run, --output-format)'. No code change required — this is a spec alignment.

improvement_plan.action: Update primary model to SD3.5 Large in architecture.json (v1.1)
Replace SDXL 1.0 in alternatives_considered with SD3.5L and Stable Image Ultra
Update region to us-west-2 with cross-region inference documentation
Formalize 3-tier model fallback and retry in C-005 component design
Add architecture benchmark research step to /architecture skill using WebSearch

completeness.notes: All prior gaps closed: RF-001 resolved (NFR-008 model quality criteria), RF-002 resolved (SC-006 image quality gate). FR-001 through FR-015 with correct priority tiers. SC-006 present. NFR-008 with Nova Canvas benchmark citations (TIFA 0.897, ImageReward 1.250, arxiv 2506.12103).

technical_soundness.notes: Model IDs accurate for us-east-1 deployment. Acceptance criteria specific and testable. Minor: timeline_weeks: 0 is an unusual value that could confuse downstream skills — better expressed as a qualitative note or removed.

well_architected.notes: Requirements implicitly address all 6 WA pillars. Security NFRs explicit (IAM, SSE-S3, S3 Block Public Access, credential management). CI/CD correctly elevated to must_have. Budget constraint removed — unconstrained is appropriate for interview PoC.

clarity.notes: Structured priority tiers (must_have/nice_to_have), specific acceptance criteria, scope boundaries explicit. FR-013/014/015 (brand/regulatory/approval) correctly labeled nice_to_have.

feasibility.notes: AI-assisted development constraint well-stated. Claude Code interface correctly scoped. Minor: timeline_weeks: 0 does not reflect the 1-week interview timeline.

overall.notes: Strong v2.0 requirements document. All prior open findings resolved. Minimal P2 finding remains (timeline_weeks). PASS — approved for downstream skills.

findings.finding: timeline_weeks: 0 in constraints is unusual. A value of 0 could be misinterpreted as 'no timeline constraint' or 'immediate'. The actual context is an interview take-home exercise with a practical deadline.
findings.recommendation: Update timeline_weeks to null or add a timeline_note: 'Interview take-home — AI-accelerated delivery, estimated 1 work day with Claude Code assistance.' This prevents downstream skills from treating 0 as a valid numeric constraint.

improvement_plan.action: Set timeline_weeks to null or add timeline_note string field

completeness.notes: C-001 through C-012 all defined with inputs, outputs, and scalability notes. 5 data flows. 3 Mermaid diagrams. Retry/backoff in C-005 (delays [2s, 4s, 8s]). S3 Block Public Access in C-008 CloudFormation spec. AnthropicBedrock client in C-012. AWS MCP servers in C-011.

technical_soundness.notes: Nova Canvas primary model well-justified with arxiv 2506.12103 citations. Model tier hierarchy (Nova Canvas→Titan V2) logical for us-east-1. AnthropicBedrock requires explicit aws_region — documented correctly. Minor: 'global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6' in tech_stack.llm is a cross-region inference ID; direct us-east-1 invocation uses 'anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6' — inconsistency with IAM section.

well_architected.notes: Parallel WA agent review (6 pillars): OpEx 6/10 — no runbooks, no CloudWatch dashboards, cache invalidation unmanaged; Security 7/10 — no Bedrock invocation logging, F-010 unresolved; Reliability 6/10 — retry delays lack jitter, no per-call timeouts, undefined RTO/RPO; Performance 6/10 — sequential generation may violate p95 NFR, no instrumentation; Cost 9/10 — tiered model strategy well-optimized for PoC; Sustainability 7/10 — us-east-1 carbon-aware but no explicit SDS calculations. Architecture self-assessed 7.7/10 — parallel review revised to 6.8/10.

clarity.notes: Component tables, data flow diagrams, and Mermaid diagrams all clear and internally consistent. Dual-audience design notes present. Decision rationale documented per component.

feasibility.notes: All 12 components achievable in stated timeframe with AI assistance. MCP server configuration is standard. Pillow text overlay coordinate tuning is the highest-uncertainty element, appropriately noted.

overall.notes: Comprehensive v2.0 architecture. Key gap: WA self-assessed scores (7.7) are materially higher than parallel agent assessment (6.8), particularly OpEx, Reliability, Performance. This discrepancy should be acknowledged in the proposal. PASS — approved for downstream skills.

findings: No runbooks or incident response procedures documented
CloudWatch metrics and dashboards not specified in C-010 (CI/CD) or C-009 (Hooks)
Local image cache in ~/.cache/pai-pipeline/ has no TTL or invalidation strategy documented
Operational Review (ORR) gate not defined before Phase 5 release
recommendations: Add CloudWatch log group for pipeline invocations to CloudFormation stack (C-008)
Document cache invalidation: either TTL-based expiry or explicit --clear-cache flag in C-001
Add basic runbook reference to C-009 (hooks) for common failure modes

findings: Bedrock invocation logging not enabled — no CloudTrail data events for model calls (F-004 open)
OIDC federation documented as preferred but Secrets-based credential alternative is the default path in deploy.yml
github.com/awslabs/mcp packages run with npx (internet-fetched at runtime) in some references
recommendations: Enable Bedrock model invocation logging to S3 (low-effort, high-value for audit)
Explicitly recommend OIDC as Phase 1 default, not alternative, in C-010 deploy workflow
Pin MCP package versions in .mcp.json to avoid supply chain risk

findings: Retry delays [2s, 4s, 8s] are deterministic — no jitter increases thundering herd risk under concurrent load
No per-call Bedrock invocation timeout documented in C-005
RTO/RPO not defined — PoC acceptable but should be explicitly stated as 'undefined for PoC'
recommendations: Add jitter to retry delays: random(0, delay_seconds) added to base delay
Document Bedrock client timeout: boto3 Config(connect_timeout=5, read_timeout=120)
State explicitly: RTO=undefined/best-effort, RPO=none (no stateful data at risk)

findings: Sequential image generation (one Bedrock call at a time) likely violates NFR p95 ≤ 60s for multi-product runs
No performance instrumentation or timing metrics in pipeline manifest
Image cache key SHA-256 computation not benchmarked against cache lookup overhead
recommendations: Add concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor for parallel image generation (documented in BACKLOG but not in C-005 spec)
Add duration_seconds per image to manifest E-006 to enable performance tracking
Note p95 NFR as 'best-effort for PoC, requires parallel execution for production'

findings: Tiered model strategy (dev/iterate/final) is well-designed for cost control
Cache strategy reduces API costs on re-runs effectively
recommendations: Add AWS Budget alarm to CloudFormation stack (F-007 finding) to prevent runaway costs during demo
Document expected per-run cost at each tier in README for transparency

findings: us-east-1 is a lower-renewable-energy region than us-west-2
No explicit SDS (Sustainability Design Specification) calculations
Cache strategy positively impacts sustainability by avoiding redundant model calls — not quantified
recommendations: Note in README that image caching reduces carbon footprint of iterative development
For production path, consider us-west-2 (higher renewable percentage) if model availability permits

wa_pillar_review.discrepancy_note: Self-assessed scores overstate OpEx, Reliability, and Performance by approximately 1-2 points each. Discrepancy is material (0.9 points overall) and should be acknowledged in the proposal's WA section. The architecture addresses all WA pillars but production-grade scores require runbooks, observability, jitter in retry, and parallelism — all appropriately deferred to Phase 4/5 or BACKLOG.

findings.finding: Model ID inconsistency: tech_stack.llm uses 'global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6' (cross-region inference format) while the IAM section and AnthropicBedrock integration reference 'anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6' (direct regional invocation). The 'global.' prefix is for cross-region inference profiles and adds latency; direct us-east-1 invocation should use the regional model ID.
findings.recommendation: Standardize to 'anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6' throughout architecture.json for us-east-1 direct invocation. Document that cross-region inference ('global.' prefix) is available as fallback if regional quota is exhausted.

findings.finding: WA self-assessed scores (OpEx 8, Reliability 8, Performance 7) are materially higher than parallel agent review (OpEx 6, Reliability 6, Performance 6). The discrepancy is 1-2 points per pillar and would be visible to an Adobe senior engineer evaluating the architecture. Key gaps: no jitter in retry, no observability instrumentation, sequential image generation.
findings.recommendation: Revise WA scores to reflect parallel agent assessment: OpEx→6, Security→7, Reliability→6, Performance→6, Cost→9, Sustainability→7, Overall→6.8. Add a note in the proposal that WA scores are PoC-level targets, with production-grade scores dependent on adding observability, parallel execution, and operational runbooks.

findings.finding: Retry delays [2s, 4s, 8s] are deterministic with no jitter. Deterministic retry intervals increase thundering herd risk in concurrent multi-user or multi-product scenarios.
findings.recommendation: Add random jitter: delay = base_delay + random(0, base_delay * 0.3). This is a one-line change in the implementation.

improvement_plan.action: Standardize Claude model ID to 'anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6' throughout architecture.json
Revise WA scores in architecture.json to reflect parallel agent review (overall 6.8)
Add jitter to retry delay specification in C-005
Add CloudWatch log group to C-008 CloudFormation spec

RF-012 identified (global. prefix inconsistency) — P1 correction needed

RF-014 noted (retry jitter missing) — P2, one-line implementation fix

feasibility.notes: Flat JSON approach is the right call for PoC. E-006 manifest schema is implementable in one Python dict. E-007 hook log adds minimal overhead.

overall.notes: Strong v2.0 data model. E-006 and E-007 correctly specified. No database references. One P2 finding: region enum mismatch. PASS.

findings.finding: E-001 (SKU Brief) region enum lists ['us', 'eu', 'asia-pacific'] but the demo briefs use 'us-west', 'latam', 'apac', 'eu' and the regulatory_checker.py lookup table (Phase 6) uses 'us-west', 'latam', 'apac', 'eu'. If the schema is enforced at runtime, demo briefs will fail validation.
findings.recommendation: Update E-001 region enum to match the runtime naming convention: ['us-west', 'eu', 'latam', 'apac']. Alternatively, add a note that region strings are free-form in v2.0 and formal enum validation is deferred to production schema.

An Adobe senior engineer conducting independent WA review may reach the same lower scores, creating a credibility gap.
findings.recommendation: Add a footnote or note in the WA section: 'Scores reflect PoC-stage self-assessment. OpEx, Reliability, and Performance targets require production additions: observability stack, parallel execution, and operational runbooks. See BACKLOG.md for production roadmap.' This demonstrates architectural honesty and awareness of the gaps.
